name: Claude Code
description: Anthropic's agentic coding assistant that operates in the terminal, capable of reading, writing, and executing code autonomously. One of the most widely discussed AI coding tools in the agent infrastructure space.

name: Andrej Karpathy
description: AI researcher and educator, formerly at OpenAI and Tesla, known for contributions to deep learning and popularizing concepts like the 'karpathy loop' for agent self-improvement.

name: OpenClaw
description: An open-source project focused on lossless context management for LLM agents, enabling efficient use of context windows without losing information.

name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
description: A technique combining retrieval systems with generative models to ground outputs in external knowledge. Includes agentic variants where agents dynamically retrieve information during reasoning.

name: Episodic Memory
description: A type of agent memory that stores specific past events, interactions, and experiences, enabling agents to recall and learn from prior episodes.

name: Cursor
description: An AI-powered code editor that integrates LLM capabilities directly into the development workflow, competing with GitHub Copilot and other coding assistants.

name: OpenAI
description: AI research company behind GPT-4, ChatGPT, Codex, and the OpenAI Agents SDK, a major driver of LLM and agent infrastructure development.

name: Anthropic
description: AI safety company behind the Claude family of models and the Model Context Protocol (MCP), focused on building reliable and interpretable AI systems.

name: AutoResearch
description: An autonomous research system (also known as Pi-AutoResearch) that iteratively generates, tests, and refines research hypotheses using LLM agents.

name: Claude
description: Anthropic's family of large language models including Claude Sonnet, Haiku, and Opus variants, widely used as the backbone for agentic systems.

name: Context Engineering
description: The discipline of designing, structuring, and optimizing the information provided to LLMs in their context window, encompassing prompt engineering, prompt architecture, and dynamic context construction.

name: Model Context Protocol
description: Anthropic's open protocol for standardizing how LLM applications communicate with external tools and data sources, enabling interoperable MCP servers.

name: Knowledge Graph
description: A structured representation of entities and their relationships used as a knowledge substrate for agents, enabling complex reasoning over interconnected information.

name: ReAct
description: Reasoning and Acting framework for LLM agents that interleaves chain-of-thought reasoning with action execution, enabling more reliable task completion.

name: Semantic Memory
description: A type of agent memory storing general world knowledge, facts, and concepts, as distinct from episodic or procedural memory.

name: LangChain
description: A popular framework for building LLM-powered applications and agents, providing abstractions for chains, tools, memory, and retrieval.

name: OpenCode
description: An open-source AI coding agent project providing an alternative to proprietary tools like Claude Code and Cursor.

name: Windsurf
description: An AI-powered IDE by Codeium that integrates agentic coding capabilities with deep codebase understanding.

name: OpenAI Codex
description: OpenAI's code generation model and CLI tool, the foundation for GitHub Copilot and various coding agent systems.

name: Agent Memory
description: The general capability of AI agents to store, retrieve, and utilize information across interactions, encompassing episodic, semantic, procedural, and core memory types.

name: Agent Skills
description: Reusable, composable capabilities that agents can acquire and invoke, including skill libraries, registries, manifests, and composition mechanisms for building capable agent systems.

name: GraphRAG
description: Microsoft's graph-based retrieval-augmented generation system that uses knowledge graphs to enable more comprehensive document understanding and question answering.

name: Progressive Disclosure
description: A context engineering strategy that reveals information incrementally based on agent needs, reducing context bloat while maintaining access to relevant details.

name: Obsidian
description: A markdown-based knowledge management tool used as a personal knowledge base, supporting wikilinks, graph views, and plugins for agent-accessible knowledge storage.

name: Long-Term Memory
description: Agent memory that persists across sessions and conversations, enabling continuity of knowledge and personalization over extended time horizons.

name: LoCoMo
description: A benchmark and framework for evaluating long-term conversational memory in AI agents across extended multi-session dialogues.

name: vLLM
description: A high-throughput inference engine for LLMs using PagedAttention, enabling efficient serving of large language models for agent systems.

name: Ollama
description: A tool for running large language models locally, enabling offline and privacy-preserving agent deployments.

name: Reflexion
description: A framework for agent self-improvement through verbal reinforcement, where agents reflect on past failures and generate improved strategies stored in memory.

name: Graphiti
description: A temporal knowledge graph system designed for agent memory, enabling agents to store and retrieve structured relational knowledge with time-awareness.

name: Zep
description: A memory platform for AI assistants and agents that provides long-term memory, summarization, and retrieval capabilities for production deployments.

name: Mem0
description: An open-source memory layer for AI agents that enables personalized, persistent memory across conversations using hybrid vector and graph storage.

name: Gemini CLI
description: Google's command-line interface for interacting with Gemini models, enabling agentic workflows from the terminal.

name: Gemini
description: Google's family of large language models used for agentic applications, available via API and integrated into various Google products.

name: GEPA
description: Generative Evolutionary Prompt Architecture - a self-improving approach that uses evolutionary algorithms to optimize agent prompts and architectures.

name: Letta
description: An agent framework formerly known as MemGPT that provides stateful agents with long-term memory, self-editing memory blocks, and a managed API for production deployments.

name: CLAUDE.md
description: A convention for storing persistent agent instructions, project context, and skill specifications in markdown files that are automatically included in agent context.

name: GRPO
description: Group Relative Policy Optimization, a reinforcement learning algorithm used to fine-tune LLMs through group-based reward comparison without a separate critic model.

name: AppWorld
description: A benchmark for evaluating LLM agents on complex multi-app tasks, simulating real-world software interactions across multiple connected applications.

name: Semantic Search
description: Search techniques that use embedding-based similarity to retrieve semantically relevant content, including vector search and vector store implementations.

name: Context Management
description: Strategies and systems for managing LLM context windows, including compression, pollution prevention, fragmentation handling, and lossless management approaches.

name: HotpotQA
description: A multi-hop question answering benchmark requiring reasoning over multiple documents, commonly used to evaluate RAG and knowledge graph systems.

name: Procedural Memory
description: A type of agent memory encoding how-to knowledge, workflows, and procedures that agents can execute, analogous to implicit skill memory in humans.

name: Multi-Agent Systems
description: Systems involving multiple AI agents that collaborate, coordinate, or compete to accomplish tasks, including workflows, orchestration, swarms, and subagent hierarchies.

name: ACE
description: Autonomous Cognitive Entity - a layered cognitive architecture framework for building autonomous agents with hierarchical reasoning and planning.

name: Vector Database
description: Specialized databases optimized for storing and querying high-dimensional embeddings, including systems like ChromaDB, Pinecone, Qdrant, FAISS, and pgvector.

name: SWE-bench
description: A benchmark for evaluating software engineering agents on real GitHub issues, with a verified subset for more reliable evaluation.

name: GPT-4
description: OpenAI's family of large language models including GPT-4, GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, and GPT-5, widely used as backbone models for agent systems.

name: BM25
description: A classical sparse retrieval algorithm used in keyword-based search, often combined with semantic search in hybrid retrieval systems for agents.

name: EvoAgentX
description: A framework for evolutionary optimization of agent architectures and workflows, enabling automated discovery of effective agent configurations.

name: AgentEvolver
description: A system for automatically evolving and improving agent capabilities through iterative optimization and evaluation cycles.

name: Tree-sitter
description: A parser generator and incremental parsing library used by coding agents for accurate code understanding and AST manipulation.

name: Human-in-the-Loop
description: An approach where human oversight, feedback, or approval is integrated into agent workflows at critical decision points.

name: Continual Learning
description: The ability of agents to continuously learn and adapt from new experiences without catastrophically forgetting previously acquired knowledge.

name: CrewAI
description: A framework for orchestrating role-playing autonomous AI agents that collaborate as a crew to accomplish complex tasks.

name: MetaGPT
description: A multi-agent framework assigning different software engineering roles to LLMs to collaboratively solve complex coding and development tasks.

name: Execution Traces
description: Records of agent actions, decisions, and intermediate states during task execution, used for debugging, evaluation, and trace-derived feedback for improvement.

name: LangGraph
description: A library for building stateful, graph-based agent workflows with LangChain, supporting cycles, persistence, and human-in-the-loop patterns.

name: LongMemEval
description: A benchmark for evaluating long-term memory capabilities in conversational AI systems across extended interaction histories.

name: Lost in the Middle
description: A phenomenon where LLMs fail to effectively use information placed in the middle of long contexts, motivating research into context organization strategies.

name: Self-Improving Agents
description: AI agents that can modify their own behavior, skills, or architecture based on experience, including self-improvement loops and open-ended evolution approaches.

name: Core Memory
description: A persistent, always-in-context memory block for agents containing essential information about the agent and user, shared across agent instances.

name: Neo4j
description: A leading graph database used for storing and querying knowledge graphs in agent memory and RAG systems.

name: ChromaDB
description: An open-source embedding database for storing and retrieving vector embeddings, commonly used in agent RAG pipelines.

name: GitHub Copilot
description: GitHub's AI coding assistant powered by OpenAI Codex and GPT-4, integrated into IDEs for code completion and generation.

name: LiteLLM
description: A unified Python SDK for calling 100+ LLM APIs using the OpenAI format, simplifying model switching in agent systems.

name: DSPy
description: A framework for programming with foundation models that automatically optimizes LLM pipelines through compiled prompt and weight optimization.

name: MemGPT
description: The original research system (now evolved into Letta) that introduced virtual context management for LLMs, enabling infinite memory through hierarchical storage.

name: Community Detection
description: Graph algorithms for identifying clusters of related entities in knowledge graphs, including the Leiden algorithm used in GraphRAG for community summarization.

name: Context Graphs
description: Graph-based representations of contextual information and agent computation flows, enabling structured context management and agentic computation graphs.

name: HyperAgents
description: A multi-agent system framework enabling hyperscale agent coordination and collaboration across distributed environments.

name: AutoGen
description: Microsoft's framework for building multi-agent conversational systems where agents can collaborate, critique, and coordinate to solve complex tasks.

name: MemEvolve
description: A system for evolving agent memory structures and retrieval strategies through automated optimization and evaluation.

name: Context Compression
description: Techniques for reducing context window usage while preserving essential information, including prompt compression methods like LLMLingua and LongLLMLingua.

name: HippoRAG
description: A RAG framework inspired by the hippocampal memory indexing theory, using knowledge graphs for more human-like associative retrieval.

name: RAPTOR
description: Recursive Abstractive Processing for Tree-Organized Retrieval - a hierarchical document indexing approach for improved multi-level retrieval in RAG systems.

name: LlamaIndex
description: A data framework for connecting LLMs with external data sources through indexing, retrieval, and query engines for RAG applications.

name: Cognitive Architecture
description: The structural organization of an AI agent's cognitive components including perception, memory, reasoning, and action, inspired by cognitive science models.

name: Agent Workflow Memory
description: A system for storing and retrieving successful agent workflows as reusable memory, enabling agents to learn from past task executions.

name: OpenMemory
description: An open-source memory layer for AI agents providing persistent, queryable memory storage across different agent frameworks and deployments.

name: SuperMemory
description: A memory system for AI agents that aggregates and makes searchable content from various sources for personalized agent memory.

name: Hybrid Search
description: A retrieval approach combining dense semantic search with sparse keyword search, often using reciprocal rank fusion to merge results for better coverage.

name: Temporal Reasoning
description: The ability of agents to reason about time-ordered events, temporal relationships, and the evolution of knowledge over time in memory systems.

name: AFlow
description: An automated workflow optimization framework that uses Monte Carlo Tree Search to discover and refine effective agent workflow patterns.

name: Voyager
description: An open-ended embodied agent in Minecraft that continuously explores, learns skills, and improves through self-directed curriculum and skill library growth.

name: Darwin Gödel Machine
description: A self-improving agent system that applies evolutionary and Gödelian self-modification principles to continuously improve its own code and capabilities.

name: Reinforcement Learning
description: A machine learning paradigm where agents learn through reward signals, applied to agent self-improvement including GRPO, reward hacking mitigation, and credit assignment.

name: ADAS
description: Automated Design of Agentic Systems - a meta-agent framework that automatically discovers and improves agent designs through code-based search.

name: TextGrad
description: A framework for automatic differentiation through text, enabling gradient-based optimization of LLM pipelines using textual feedback as gradients.

name: WebArena
description: A realistic web environment benchmark for evaluating autonomous agents on complex web-based tasks across multiple websites.

name: Mind2Web
description: A benchmark for generalist web agents that tests task completion across diverse real-world websites and user instructions.

name: SkillBook
description: A structured registry or book of agent skills and their specifications, including SkillManager systems for organizing and accessing agent capabilities.

name: LightRAG
description: A lightweight graph-based RAG system that integrates entity and relationship extraction into retrieval for improved contextual responses.

name: MemoryBank
description: A long-term memory mechanism for LLMs that stores and retrieves memories based on Ebbinghaus forgetting curve principles for more human-like memory management.

name: SkillWeaver
description: A system for automated skill synthesis and composition in agents, enabling learning and weaving together new skills from experience.

name: Tau-bench
description: A benchmark for evaluating LLM agents on complex tool-augmented tasks, including Tau3 for multi-step reasoning evaluation.

name: Chain-of-Thought
description: A prompting technique that elicits step-by-step reasoning from LLMs, including tool-integrated reasoning variants where reasoning is interleaved with tool calls.

name: Synthetic Data Generation
description: Techniques for creating artificial training data for agent improvement, including frontier-to-local distillation where larger models generate data for smaller ones.

name: Lilian Weng
description: OpenAI researcher and author of influential blog posts on LLM agents, memory systems, and agent architectures.

name: Jeff Clune
description: AI researcher known for work on open-ended learning, quality-diversity algorithms, and self-improving AI systems.

name: OpenAI Agents SDK
description: OpenAI's SDK for building production-ready agentic applications, providing abstractions for tools, handoffs, and agent coordination, merged with Claude Agent SDK concepts.

name: Zettelkasten
description: A note-taking and knowledge management methodology using atomic linked notes, adapted for agent knowledge bases through markdown wikis and wikilinks.

name: Aider
description: An open-source AI pair programming tool that enables LLMs to collaboratively edit code in local git repositories via the command line.

name: AutoGPT
description: One of the first widely-adopted autonomous agent frameworks, enabling LLMs to decompose and pursue long-horizon goals with tool use.

name: OSWorld
description: A benchmark for evaluating computer-use agents on real-world OS tasks across multiple operating system environments.

name: RAGFlow
description: An open-source RAG engine with deep document understanding capabilities for building enterprise knowledge retrieval systems.

name: ElizaOS
description: An open-source framework for building conversational AI agents with persistent memory, multi-platform support, and plugin architecture.

name: Cognee
description: A knowledge graph construction and retrieval system for AI agents that transforms unstructured data into queryable semantic networks.

name: Memento
description: A memory and skills management system for AI agents combining persistent storage with skill tracking capabilities.

name: A-MEM
description: An agentic memory system that autonomously organizes and evolves memory structures based on agent interaction patterns.

name: Prompt Optimization
description: Automated techniques for improving prompt quality and agent harness configurations, including meta-prompt optimization and automated harness optimization.

name: Organizational Memory
description: Knowledge and information that persists within organizations or agent systems beyond individual interactions, including institutional memory and organizational judgment.

name: Case-Based Reasoning
description: A problem-solving approach that uses experiences from similar past cases to inform new decisions, applicable to agent memory and retrieval systems.

name: Pi
description: A personal AI assistant project focused on natural conversation and emotional intelligence, referenced in agent infrastructure discussions.

name: SEAgent
description: A self-evolving agent system that iteratively improves its capabilities through automated evaluation and optimization cycles.

name: Workflow Induction
description: The process of automatically inferring or learning effective agent workflows from demonstrations, traces, or experience.

name: Incremental Indexing
description: A knowledge base maintenance strategy that updates indexes progressively as new information arrives, avoiding full reindexing overhead.

name: Short-Term Memory
description: In-context memory available during a single agent session, including mechanisms for context retrieval and context reuse within the active window.

name: LLM-as-Judge
description: Using LLMs as automated evaluators to score or rank agent outputs, enabling scalable evaluation functions for self-improvement pipelines.

name: Personalized PageRank
description: A graph algorithm variant used in knowledge graph retrieval to find relevant nodes by propagating relevance scores from query entities.

name: Entity Extraction
description: NLP technique for identifying and extracting named entities and relationships from text, foundational to knowledge graph construction for agent memory.

name: Agent Harness
description: The scaffolding and evaluation infrastructure surrounding an agent, including meta-harness configurations, model harnesses, and agentic evaluation frameworks.

name: Docker Sandbox
description: Containerized execution environments providing safe, isolated sandboxes for agents to run code and system commands.

name: Observability
description: Systems for monitoring, tracing, and debugging agent behavior in production, including tools like LangSmith and error logging infrastructure.

name: Memory Evolution
description: The process by which agent memory systems adapt, reorganize, and improve over time through experience and automated optimization.

name: World Model
description: An agent's internal representation of the environment, entities, and their relationships, used for planning and decision-making.

name: DeepSeek
description: A Chinese AI lab's family of open-source language models including DeepSeek-R1, widely adopted for agent applications due to strong reasoning capabilities.

name: Compositional Skill Synthesis
description: An approach to building agent capabilities by composing and synthesizing skills from simpler primitives, including automatic curriculum generation for skill acquisition.

name: Markdown Wiki
description: A knowledge base format using interconnected markdown files as a lightweight, human-readable knowledge substrate for agents and teams.

name: System Prompt
description: The initial instruction context provided to LLMs that defines agent behavior, persona, and constraints before user interaction begins.

name: Meta-Agent
description: A higher-level agent that oversees, coordinates, or improves other agents, taking on roles such as architect, curator, or reflector in multi-agent systems.

name: Tool Registry
description: A catalog of available tools and their specifications that agents can query and use, enabling dynamic tool discovery and selection.

name: Pinecone
description: A managed vector database service widely used for storing and querying embeddings in production RAG and agent memory systems.

name: Structured Output
description: Techniques for constraining LLM outputs to follow specific schemas or formats, enabling reliable parsing and downstream processing in agent pipelines.

name: Letta API
description: The managed API layer of the Letta platform providing hosted stateful agent memory services for production deployments.

name: GAIA
description: A benchmark for evaluating general AI assistants on real-world tasks requiring multi-step reasoning, tool use, and information retrieval.

name: HumanEval
description: A benchmark for evaluating code generation capabilities of LLMs, often used alongside MBPP for assessing coding agent performance.

name: Antigravity
description: An agent infrastructure project referenced in the context of agent architecture and tool development.

name: Docling
description: IBM's document parsing library for converting PDFs and other document formats into structured data suitable for RAG and knowledge bases.

name: MARP
description: Markdown Presentation Ecosystem for creating presentations from markdown, used in knowledge management and documentation workflows.

name: Agent Zero
description: A general-purpose autonomous agent framework that uses a zero-shot approach to task decomposition and execution with minimal predefined structure.

name: MGX
description: MetaGPT's multi-agent collaboration platform enabling team-based AI workflows for complex software and product development tasks.

name: TerminalBench
description: A benchmark for evaluating terminal-based agent capabilities on complex command-line tasks, with TerminalBench-2 as an updated version.

name: MemVid
description: A video-based memory system for agents that encodes memories as video frames for compact, retrievable long-term storage.

name: MinerU
description: An open-source document extraction tool for converting PDFs and web pages into structured markdown for knowledge base ingestion.

name: Cerebra Fusion Memory
description: A memory fusion system for agents that combines multiple memory modalities for more comprehensive and accessible agent memory.

name: SuperAGI
description: An open-source autonomous AI agent framework with a graphical interface for building, managing, and running multiple concurrent agents.

name: Context Generation
description: The process of dynamically generating relevant context for agents through autocontext and acontext systems that identify and surface pertinent information.

name: Agent-Mediated Workflows
description: Business and technical processes orchestrated by AI agents, replacing or augmenting traditional automated workflows with adaptive agentic execution.

name: Compound Engineering
description: An engineering approach that builds AI systems as compound products combining multiple models, tools, and services rather than relying on a single model.

name: ReMe
description: A retrieval-enhanced memory system for conversational agents that improves recall through structured memory organization and retrieval.

name: Bounded Context
description: A domain-driven design principle applied to agent context management, limiting context scope to relevant domain boundaries for efficiency.

name: Loop Detection
description: Mechanisms for detecting and breaking infinite loops or unproductive cycles in agent execution, including lifecycle hooks for intervention.

name: Elasticsearch
description: A distributed search and analytics engine used for full-text and hybrid search in agent knowledge retrieval systems.

name: Multi-hop RAG
description: RAG approaches requiring multiple retrieval steps to answer complex questions, chaining retrievals across documents to build complete answers.

name: Dynamic Cheatsheet
description: An adaptive reference document that agents maintain and update with learned heuristics, patterns, and useful information during task execution.

name: Continual RAG
description: RAG systems that continuously update their knowledge bases with new information while maintaining retrieval performance without full reindexing.

name: Zero-Data Self-Evolution
description: A self-improvement approach where agents evolve their capabilities without external training data, relying on self-generated experience and evaluation.

name: Tool-Integrated Reasoning
description: A reasoning paradigm where LLM agents interleave reasoning steps with tool calls, enabling grounded and verifiable problem solving.

name: SQLAlchemy
description: A Python SQL toolkit and ORM used in agent infrastructure for structured data storage and retrieval.

name: Coral
description: A multi-agent collaboration platform enabling structured agent-to-agent communication and workflow coordination.

name: Pydantic
description: A Python data validation library widely used in agent systems for defining and validating structured tool inputs and outputs.

name: NetworkX
description: A Python library for creating and analyzing graphs, used in knowledge graph construction and community detection for agent memory systems.

name: Claude-Mem
description: A memory extension system for Claude that provides persistent memory capabilities across Claude conversations and sessions.

name: OpenViking
description: An open-source agent project contributing to the agent infrastructure ecosystem.

name: GStack
description: An agent infrastructure stack project for building and deploying AI agent systems.

name: Kuzu
description: An embeddable property graph database designed for high-performance graph queries in agent knowledge systems.

name: SAGE
description: A structured agent execution framework for building reliable and observable agentic pipelines.

name: WebShop
description: A simulated e-commerce environment for training and evaluating web-based shopping agents on product search and selection tasks.

name: Perplexity
description: An AI-powered search engine that combines LLM generation with real-time web retrieval, serving as both a product and a benchmark reference.

name: Qwen
description: Alibaba's family of open-source large language models including Qwen2.5, used as backbone models for various agent applications.

name: Jack Dorsey
description: Co-founder of Twitter and Square, referenced in discussions about AI agents in enterprise and product contexts.

name: Jaya Gupta
description: Contributor and researcher in the agent infrastructure and memory systems space.

name: Udit Goenka
description: Builder and contributor in the AI agent ecosystem, referenced in agent infrastructure discussions.

name: Garry Tan
description: President and CEO of Y Combinator, referenced in discussions about AI agent adoption in startups.

name: Tobi Lütke
description: CEO of Shopify, known for early adoption of AI agents in enterprise workflows and advocacy for agent-first product development.

name: Lossless Context Management
description: A context management approach that preserves all relevant information when handling long contexts, avoiding information loss through intelligent summarization and retrieval.

name: Agent Learning
description: The processes by which agents acquire new knowledge and capabilities through interaction, feedback, and experience, including in-context and parametric learning approaches.

name: Hipocampus
description: A memory system project inspired by the hippocampus, providing associative memory retrieval capabilities for AI agents.

name: SQLite
description: A lightweight embedded relational database widely used in agent systems for local structured data storage and retrieval.

name: PostgreSQL
description: A powerful open-source relational database used in agent infrastructure for structured data storage, often with pgvector for embedding support.

name: Redis
description: An in-memory data structure store used in agent systems for caching, pub/sub messaging, and fast key-value storage.

name: FAISS
description: Facebook AI Similarity Search - a library for efficient similarity search and clustering of dense vectors, used in agent memory retrieval systems.

name: Qdrant
description: An open-source vector similarity search engine designed for production-scale agent memory and RAG applications.

name: LanceDB
description: An embedded vector database built on the Lance columnar format, optimized for multimodal AI applications and agent memory.

name: LangSmith
description: LangChain's observability and evaluation platform for monitoring, debugging, and testing LLM agent applications in production.

name: GPT-4o
description: OpenAI's omni model variant of GPT-4 with improved multimodal capabilities, widely used as an agent backbone.

name: Notion
description: A collaborative workspace tool used as a knowledge base and documentation platform in agent-assisted workflows.

name: ChatGPT
description: OpenAI's conversational AI product built on GPT-4, widely referenced as a baseline for agent capability comparisons.

name: Groq
description: A hardware and inference company providing ultra-fast LLM inference using LPU chips, used in latency-sensitive agent applications.

name: llama.cpp
description: A C++ implementation of LLaMA inference enabling efficient local LLM execution for edge agent deployments.

name: FinanceBench
description: A benchmark for evaluating LLM and RAG systems on financial document question answering tasks.

name: Agentic RAG
description: A RAG variant where agents dynamically plan and execute multi-step retrieval strategies, adapting queries based on intermediate results.

name: Cypher
description: A declarative graph query language used with Neo4j and other graph databases for querying knowledge graphs in agent memory systems.

name: Context Database
description: A specialized database for storing and retrieving agent context, enabling persistent and queryable context management across sessions.

name: Memori
description: A memory service for AI agents providing cloud-based persistent memory storage and retrieval capabilities.

name: LightRAG
description: A lightweight graph-enhanced RAG framework combining entity extraction with dual-level retrieval for improved knowledge graph-based responses.

name: Block
description: Jack Dorsey's financial technology company (formerly Square), referenced in enterprise AI agent adoption discussions.

name: EvolveLab
description: A research project or lab focused on evolutionary approaches to improving AI agent capabilities and architectures.

name: SuperMemory
description: A universal memory layer for AI systems aggregating bookmarks, documents, and web content into a searchable agent memory store.

name: Pinecone
description: A fully managed vector database for ML applications, commonly used for semantic search and agent long-term memory.

name: Agent0
description: An agent framework or baseline system referenced in agent architecture comparisons and evaluations.

name: SEAgent
description: A self-evolving agent framework that uses automated evaluation loops to continuously improve agent performance on target tasks.

name: Docling
description: An open-source document parsing tool by IBM for converting complex documents into structured formats for RAG pipelines.

name: LLMLingua
description: A prompt compression framework from Microsoft that uses smaller LLMs to compress prompts while preserving semantic meaning for more efficient inference.

name: Token Efficiency
description: Strategies and metrics for minimizing token usage in agent interactions while maintaining task performance, including attention budget management.

name: WebShop
description: An interactive web shopping benchmark environment for evaluating agent task completion in simulated e-commerce settings.

name: Multi-Agent Collaboration
description: Patterns and protocols for multiple AI agents working together, sharing information, and coordinating actions toward common goals.

name: OpenMementos
description: A project related to agent memory management, likely inspired by or extending the Memento concept for tracking and retrieving past agent states or interactions.

name: OpenThoughts-v3
description: An open-source dataset or model focused on reasoning and chain-of-thought capabilities, representing the third iteration of the OpenThoughts series.

name: QwQ-32B
description: A 32-billion parameter reasoning-focused language model from the Qwen family, designed for extended chain-of-thought reasoning tasks.

name: AI Frontiers
description: A research initiative or conference focused on frontier AI research and development.

name: Qwen2.5-7B
description: A 7-billion parameter language model from Alibaba's Qwen2.5 series, used as a base or fine-tuned model for various agent and reasoning tasks.

name: Qwen3-8B
description: An 8-billion parameter language model from Alibaba's Qwen3 series, part of the third generation of Qwen models.

name: Qwen3-32B
description: A 32-billion parameter language model from Alibaba's Qwen3 series, offering stronger reasoning and instruction-following capabilities.

name: Phi-4-Reasoning
description: Microsoft's Phi-4 model variant fine-tuned specifically for reasoning tasks, part of the Phi small language model family.

name: OLMo3-7B-Think
description: A 7-billion parameter thinking-oriented variant of Allen AI's OLMo3 open language model, designed for extended reasoning.

name: KV Cache
description: Key-Value cache used in transformer inference to store intermediate attention computations, enabling faster generation by avoiding redundant computation for previously processed tokens.

name: Block Masking
description: A technique in transformer attention that selectively masks entire blocks of tokens, used for efficient context management or training objectives like memory segmentation.

name: Memento (Concept)
description: A conceptual framework for agent memory inspired by the film Memento, emphasizing backward-chaining recall and the use of external memory artifacts to compensate for limited in-context memory.

name: Supervised Fine-Tuning
description: A training approach where a pre-trained language model is further trained on labeled examples to improve performance on specific tasks or domains.

name: Dynamic Programming
description: An algorithmic optimization technique that solves complex problems by breaking them into overlapping subproblems and storing solutions, applied in agent planning and memory retrieval optimization.

name: Dimitris Papailiopoulos
description: Researcher working on machine learning and AI, known for contributions to memory systems and self-improving agents.

name: Vasilis Kontonis
description: Researcher in machine learning and AI systems.

name: Yuchen Zeng
description: Researcher contributing to LLM agent and memory systems research.

name: Shivam Garg
description: Researcher in machine learning and AI systems.

name: Lingjiao Chen
description: Researcher working on AI systems and language model infrastructure.

name: Hao Tang
description: Researcher contributing to AI and language model systems research.

name: Ziyan Wang
description: Researcher in AI systems and language model development.

name: Ahmed Awadallah
description: Senior researcher at Microsoft Research working on natural language processing and AI systems.

name: Eric Horvitz
description: Chief Scientific Officer at Microsoft, known for foundational work in AI decision-making, probabilistic reasoning, and human-AI interaction.

name: John Langford
description: Machine learning researcher at Microsoft Research known for contributions to reinforcement learning and online learning algorithms.

name: AIME 2026
description: The 2026 American Invitational Mathematics Examination, used as a benchmark for evaluating mathematical reasoning capabilities of language models.

name: GPQA-Diamond
description: The Diamond subset of the Graduate-Level Google-Proof Q&A benchmark, consisting of extremely challenging expert-level science questions used to evaluate frontier model reasoning.

name: LiveCodeBench
description: A benchmark for evaluating code generation capabilities of language models using live, continuously updated coding problems to prevent data contamination.
